title: Arachnorchis oenochila
taxonomy: Arachnorchis oenochila is classified as a species within the genus Arachnorchis, which is a group of orchid plants native to Australia. The genus Arachnorchis is part of the subfamily Orchidoideae, within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. The subfamily Orchidoideae is further grouped into the tribe Diurideae, which comprises several species of terrestrial orchids found primarily in Australia and New Zealand. The tribe Diurideae is thought to have evolved from a common ancestor around 15-20 million years ago. Arachnorchis oenochila's exact classification is still subject to ongoing research and possible revisions.

intro: Arachnorchis oenochila is a species of terrestrial orchid found in the wild, commonly known as the greenhood orchid due to the green color of its labellum. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to promote health and well-being, with reported benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties that may aid in wound healing and reduce the severity of cold and flu symptoms. The bioactive constituents of Arachnorchis oenochila include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and it can also be found in dried form or as a standardized extract, allowing for various forms of administration.
